meta.comment:
v0.11 (mixed-holdout): v0.9's deployed-model set plus direct RouterArena label entries (bench_column=routerarena_<model>) for the 9 candidate models we labeled via routerarena_label.py. Proxy entries are kept so the trainer can union OpenRouterBench rows with RouterArena rows; pure RouterArena variants (v0.13/v0.14) use --routerarena-only at training time and ignore the proxy columns implicitly. New deployed model: gpt-5.4-mini, the cheap-OpenAI tier we substituted for the (non-existent on the API) gpt-5.5-mini. It has no OpenRouterBench column today, so it relies on its direct RouterArena column for coverage — fine because every cluster will have RouterArena observations under sub_10/full split coverage.

Why duplicates are safe: bench_walker keys on (prompt, deployed_model). The two source domains (OpenRouterBench prompts, RouterArena prompt_text) do not overlap in practice, so each record contributes once to its (cluster, model) cell. The runtime dedupe fix in scorer.go (D10) is what made multi-entry registries safe in v0.6+.

deployed_models.proxy_note: no claude-haiku column in OpenRouterBench or SWE-bench experiments; gemini-2.5-flash stands in as the cheapest small-tier proxy until D3 traffic accumulates
no claude-opus-4-7 column in OpenRouterBench; gpt-5 is a frontier proxy
v0.3-new: closer Anthropic-family proxy from SWE-bench experiments
GPT-5.5 (April 2026) ships after the bench cutoff; use gpt-5's column as the closest generational proxy
v0.11-new: cheap-OpenAI candidate. No OpenRouterBench column; RouterArena direct label is the only source. Substituted for gpt-5.5-mini, which is not available on the OpenAI API.
v0.8: gemini-2.5-pro entry dropped — see v0.9 meta.comment. Remaining proxy column is SWE-bench-derived gemini-3-pro-preview, generationally close (preview-vs-preview)
Gemini 3 Flash Preview shipped post-bench; use gemini-2.5-flash's column
Cheapest Google entry; same column as gemini-3-flash-preview but lower cost rebalances the α-blend toward this tier
